project.name: OrtoBolt

tech_stack.ai: Gemini/Claude (via custom proxy), Zod for validation

project.description: A sophisticated AI-powered clinical tool for veterinary orthopedics, combining an advanced frontend with serverless AI routing and a Supabase backend for case management and RAG.

health.comments: The project uses a bleeding-edge stack and is stable in build, but suffers from a 'God Object' context and fragmented AI orchestration that will hinder long-term maintenance.

findings.impact: Publicly accessible endpoint allowing unauthorized use of GEMINI_API_KEY.
Bypasable rate limits due to Vercel serverless cold starts.
Localhost origins allowed in production config.
Full table fetches (.select('*')) will degrade as data grows.
Any state change in AppContext triggers app-wide re-renders.
O(N) operations in render path causing potential UI lag.
Logic split between aiService and ortoboltEngine makes maintenance difficult.
Frontend triggers embeddings, increasing latency and round-trips.

high_priority: Refactor AppContext.tsx to remove 'God Object' pattern.
Implement pagination for Supabase case and notification fetches.
medium_priority: Unify AI orchestration into a single module/class.
Move RAG embedding and retrieval logic to the backend proxy.
Implement a distributed rate limiter (e.g., Upstash Redis).
low_priority: Extract common SSE streaming logic into a helper function.
Move system prompts to external configuration files.
Replace simple Map cache with an LRU cache.
recommendations: Immediately secure the embeddings API with JWT verification.
Decompose the global AppContext into specialized contexts (Auth, Case, UI).
Optimize DashboardPage by reducing the case list into buckets in a single pass.
roadmap: Phase 1: Security Hardening (Auth on all API endpoints, CORS cleanup).
Phase 2: State & Performance Optimization (Context splitting, API pagination).
Phase 3: AI Architectural Refinement (Backend RAG, Unified Orchestrator, External Prompts).
Phase 4: DX Improvements (DRY streaming logic, LRU caching).

bundle_sizes.others: vendor-core: 176.36 kB, vendor-pdf: 391.64 kB, vendor-charts: 367.64 kB
